transcript.text: Hello, how's it going?
I'm good, how are you?
I'm well as well, thank you.
Happy Wednesday.
Happy Wednesday.
Are you based in the US?
I am.
I'm in DC at the moment, but I moved back to LA in January.
Oh, did you get yesterday off?
Yeah.
It was more like with Fleet, they say, like they've given us two options.
You can either have like the traditional like holidays off or pick your own holidays.
Like take your, like I'll work 4th of July, but then I'll just take the Friday off or something like that.
So we just give them a heads up, which is great.
Yeah, that's nice.
Here we can, because we're like an international team, so everyone can choose to celebrate their own national holidays.
But the first half of this year, I was like, no, I think I'm going to celebrate like US holidays just because it's easier for us.
Work.
And then halfway through, I was like, no, actually, I should just take my own national holidays.
But the issue is that most of my country's national holidays are in the first half of the year.
So I was just, now I feel like I should be celebrating Thanksgiving.
You're like, I want to be included in that.
Yeah, yeah.
So I kind of played myself a little bit, but it's no big deal.
Yeah.
So in LA, there's like a big Australian community.
So they celebrate like Australia Day and they always do like a big, like there's a couple of Aussie bars and restaurants that will just like shut down and just open the place up to all the Aussies.
And it's like one of my, it's one of my favorite days because everyone just kind of comes together and you find, oh, yep, we're all in the same boat.
So I think that might be a day I might just be like, guys, I need to not work today.
When is Australia Day?
Um, it's January 26th.
26th or 27th.
It's the day that Kobe died.
That gives me a Because I'm like, I can't remember which date it is, but I'm like, every American knows the day Kobe died.
So I'm like, it was that day.
Wow.
Yeah, yeah, that's the, yeah, I have a similar thing with Queen Elizabeth.
I had a surgery on the day that she died.
So it was like, I always remember that day.
I always remember the date.
Yeah.
All right, cool.
I think it's just going to be you, right?
I think Brock's out.
So I think we could get started.
So I did add you to the agenda, so you should get it in your inbox.
But to do a quick content update.
So we're working on three articles this week.
Next week, we want to do five.
But for...
This week's batch, this one is already ready for reviews, so what is Apple's device enrollment program?
I'll send it in our Slack channel, but if you also go to our Airtable, if you go to the, sorry, Airtable is notoriously slow.
If you go to Fleet Reviewing Drafts, you can find the Google Doc links to everything that's in review.
Amazing.
Yeah.
Did you get a chance to take a look at the blogs that we sent over so far?
I unfortunately have not.
think Brock did.
So I haven't touched base with him.
I believe he's in Europe right now, I want to say.
Okay, okay.
So I'll schedule off as well.
Okay, okay.
That's totally fine.
We'll just keep going, and then any feedback that you have, we can kind of implement it after the fact, so.
Sounds good.
Yeah.
All right.
And then we also.
So we do need additional topics to be approved for next week.
We did ping Brock about it yesterday or two days ago, but in case you also want to go through and leave feedback, you're more than welcome to.
So if you, let me just group them by cluster so it's easier.
Right, so they're all grouped into different clusters that you can find in our content strategy.
We have around 60 right now.
So you can leave comments by expanding the cell and then you can just drop a comment here.
Let me know if you need the link to this base.
It should be in Notion.
Great.
Okay, just, yeah, I'll just put in the chat just in case I, for some reason.
They Um.
Elite enca I love it.
I kalian.
Okay.
Awesome.
And we are, I checked, so we still haven't gotten access to Google Search Console.
I don't know who has ownership of that.
Like, it's just a matter of, I think, adding our team email to the account.
Yeah, I mentioned it to Sam last week.
He said he was going to check with somebody, but I will send a quick reminder.
Okay.
Okay.
That makes sense.
I mean, we do, we have this, like, Looker dashboard built.
It's a multi-page report that combines data from both platforms.
So if we just do GA4, it's kind of like half of the graphs don't work.
So it doesn't, like, it's not as helpful as it is when you have, like, both platforms in there.
But we do have, and I don't know, Erik, I don't think we've gone through Scrunch yet.
I remember talking about it, but I don't know if we went through it.
Yeah, because I feel like every meeting we had, we talked about different things, and I never got around to presenting Scrunch.
So I think we can, if you do remember this, let me know, Irina, because I don't want to, but this is the Scrunch dashboard.
So this is an AI visibility tool.
It's one of many on the market.
It's similar to Profound, if you've heard of it.
So what they do is we give them a description of your company, we give it a description of your audience, and then we also provide a list of competitors.
And then based on those inputs, they generate base prompts that they use to send to different LLMs, like the ones that they have right here.
So like ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and then they collect the responses, they analyze.
And then they present the data into Dashboard.
So it's kind of like just trying to kind of reverse engineer what your audience is doing in LLMs because LLMs, they don't share their user data with anybody else.
So this is kind of a way to get around it.
Erik, did you add?
I think you added more prompts here, right?
I did last week.
Okay, great.
So yeah, usually like we have around 150, I think, base prompts, but then we also generate additional ones using your artifacts that we also add in here.
But we can track like thousands of prompts and, you know, depending on, I mean, we can share the file with you that contains all of the prompts.
And if you want to add any, you can let us know if there are any questions that repeatedly come up from your customers.
We can also use those questions to generate prompts and then put them in the Dashboard.
I think that's probably the closest.
noteless.
It's be
We can get to, yeah, like actual, like user behavior.
So, but yeah, I think with, so just to give you kind of an overview of what everything is.
So this monitors your overall competitive presence over time.
So it has been, for the prompts that we're tracking, it has been growing over the past couple of weeks.
So right now you're second behind Microsoft, your overall position in these responses is more towards the middle.
So for example, if ChatGPT is listing off different products, like you would be in the middle.
The sentiment is mostly positive, and you do get 1% of citations, which is not that bad, considering for most of our clients, we see a citation rate of around 3% to 4%.
Um,
So the more, I mean, your competitors have a much, much higher citation rate, but it's because they have much bigger content libraries.

So, for example, we have one client in cybersecurity who has a blog, they have like a resource library with just exclusively like SEO content, I mean, like a more like foundational educational content.
And they have like a 15% citation rate just because there's just so much information that LLMs can use in their responses.
So the more content you have, the more citations you get.
I do have to point out that LLMs have been like updating their algorithms pretty often, and that impacts what kind of websites are cited.
So this summer, they pushed, like ChatGPT pushed a really big update that started favoring Wikipedia and Reddit inside.
But it looks like I think as of maybe last month, that that has changed.
So now they're kind of pulling from different sources.
They're not like favoring just Reddit.
For a while, Reddit was like one of the top sources when it came to citations.
And now it's a little bit more balanced.
But yeah, think the more, generally speaking, the more educational content the website has, the more, like the greater the likelihood it's going to be cited.
Especially competitive comparisons, like versus content, listicles, comparing different solutions, like best tool for X.
And then also like how-to guides, that I also get surfaced a lot.
If we go to prompts, I can kind of show you what are the topics.
So Scrunch automatically categorizes all of our prompts into different topics.
Just by category, so for example, for, let's see which ones do we want to pick, let's go with Device Management Best Practices.
So these are all the seed prompts that we have in this topic.
And then if we like click on this one, you can see the individual data for this prompt.
So for example, in Perplexity, you appeared pretty much for every single prompt over the last week, I think, or like two weeks.
Claude, generally speaking, doesn't cite that often, but Perplexity does provide citations more often than Claude.
But this kind of gives us an insight into what are the topics where you tend to show

Go up where are the topics that your competitors have a higher presence, and then it allows us also to kind of tailor the content strategy if there's a specific topic area where your visibility is going down or you don't have a good visibility, we can kind of try to do additional content research and like generate topics that support that type of visibility.
If it's just a dot, then that means that we added the prompt recently, so it doesn't have enough data to go off of.
So that's just wanted to flag that.
It's not that it's bad, but it's just the prompt was added here recently.
And then this is kind of, this data, we complement it with data in your Looker dashboard.
So we have an LLM referral report built in Looker on the last page, and it breaks down all of.

The different LLMs that are sending traffic to your site, and we can see, okay, like, usually it's ChatGPT and perplexity that are driving the most traffic to our client sites, and then we can also see what are the URLs that are getting the most traffic from different LLMs.

So this kind of, these two reports complement each other, because this gives us an insight into your competitors, while, like, the Looker report actually lets us see the actual data, like, what's the, what are the pages they get the most visibility.

And I do have to point out that for our customers, especially those that come to us with, like, a really big content library already, they're, the majority of the referral traffic is going to their blog.
So it's, like, usually the homepage, maybe, like, a product page, and then it's just, like, the blog.
And we can export this dashboard as like a PDF and send it to you, so you can kind of, yeah, use it for whatever you need.
But yeah, you have any questions around just scrunch or AI visibility tracking, happy to answer.
Is this something we, like, is this something you guys just have access to, or is there a way that we can, like, access this page as well with, the data?
Or would you just have to send the PDF page?
Yeah, right now we can just send you the PDF because it's limited to cross- emails, but I'll look into, let's see if we could do something about that.
I just feel like our head of demand, Jen, would have, would love to get his eyes on this.
thought he was going to be here today, but I think maybe we didn't send him, I didn't send him the link.
Yeah, that tracks.
Yeah.
Okay, cool.
Cool, cool.
All right, great.
Yeah, I think we can share, we can share the PDF report, we can share the prompts, and then I'm sure that, you know, he can have, he's going to have, like, ideas for what we can add in the prompts.

Like said, we can track, like, So, the more, you know, some of the prompts, they can be quite specific, the ones that we were tracking, and they're kind of, you know, we just want to avoid leading the witness, you know, basically, like, trying to nudge the LLM to, so that's what we want to avoid, want to keep them, like, related, obviously, to our industry and the, the solution, but also general enough, so that it's similar to what a user would actually be looking for.
Gotcha.
Yeah.
Okay, I think those are all the updates that we had, right, Erik?
I believe so, yeah.
Okay, if you don't,
If any questions, I think we can wrap up early.
Okay.
Yeah.
Oops, sorry.
Go on.
No, no, no.
Go ahead.
No, I was just going to say, so from us, you needed Brock and I to go through and pick out five more topics for next week, correct?
You could do more if you have time.
Okay.

I think 15 would be ideal, just so we have, like, we like to build up a backlog in case someone's out of office or sick, so just to have, like, a head start, but, and then review the two articles that are in C-reviewing.
Perfect.
Okay, we'll do.
Okay.
Perfect.
Thank you so much.
Uni, thank you so much.
I appreciate both of you's time.
See you next week.
Bye-bye.
See you next week.
Bye, guys.-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content progress and review AI visibility tracking.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Pipeline:** One article is ready for review. The next goal is 5 articles/week, requiring approval of \~15 topics to build a production backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)
  - **AI Visibility:** Fleet ranks \#2 behind Microsoft in Scrunch, an AI visibility tool. The low 1% citation rate (vs. 3-4% client average) is due to a smaller content library.
  - [**Data Blocker:** Google Search Console (GSC) access is still pending. This prevents a key Looker dashboard from functioning, as it requires data from both GSC and GA4.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=304.0)

## Topics

### Content Production & Review

  - [**Current Production:** 3 articles this week.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)
  - [**Review Pipeline:**](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=178.0)
      - ["What is Apple's device enrollment program?" → Ready for review.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)
      - [Find all review drafts in the "Fleet Reviewing Drafts" Airtable view.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=178.0)
  - [**Next Week's Goal:** 5 articles.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)
      - [Requires approving \~15 topics from the content strategy Airtable.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=968.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Build a production backlog to maintain momentum during team absences.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=968.0)

### AI Visibility Tracking (Scrunch)

  - [**Tool:** Scrunch, an AI visibility tool that reverse-engineers LLM responses (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity) to track competitive presence.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=363.0)
  - [**Fleet's Performance:**](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=486.0)
      - **Rank:** \#2 overall, behind Microsoft.
      - [**Position:** Middle of LLM-generated product lists.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=492.0)
      - [**Citation Rate:** 1%.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=492.0)
          - [**Significance:** This is lower than the 3-4% client average.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=492.0)
          - [**Cause:** Fleet's smaller content library. More educational content (how-to guides, comparisons) is needed to increase citations.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=524.0)
  - [**Data Complement:** Scrunch data is paired with a Looker dashboard that tracks actual LLM referral traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=762.0)
      - [**Blocker:** This dashboard is incomplete without GSC access.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=304.0)
  - [**Access:** Scrunch is currently internal to GrowthX. A PDF report will be shared with Fleet's Head of Demand, Jen.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=862.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Irena & Brock:**](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=968.0)
      - [Review the article "What is Apple's device enrollment program?"](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=178.0)
      - [Approve \~15 topics in the content strategy Airtable to build a production backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=968.0)
  - [**Erik:**](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=304.0)
      - [Follow up with Sam to secure Google Search Console access.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=304.0)
  - [**Aida:**](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=862.0)
      - [Share the Scrunch PDF report with Irena for Jen's review.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=862.0)
      - [Investigate direct Scrunch access for Fleet.](https://fathom.video/share/beS8crdSPCSx33kbtDgwLKDGzt92tpzj?tab=summary&timestamp=862.0)

action_items.description: Post 'What is Apple's DEP?' review link in Slack for Irena
Schedule sync w/ Brock re: blog reviews
Remind Sam re: add GrowthX team email to Google Search Console
Investigate cross-org Scrunch access; update Irena
Send Scrunch PDF + prompt list to Irena
Review 2 articles in Fleet Reviewing Drafts
